nodes.text: You are a senior accountant. You discover your mentor, David, has been embezzling funds. If you report him, he goes to jail and his family is ruined. If you stay silent, the company could collapse.

choices.text: Report him to HR immediately.
Confront him privately and tell him to put it back.
Copy the logs to an external drive as insurance, say nothing, and wait.

nodes.text: HR thanks you, but the CEO calls you into his office. He says David is too important to the company's image. He offers you a massive promotion to bury the evidence.

choices.text: Accept the promotion and bury it.
Refuse and threaten to go to the press.
Delay the CEO. Say you need a few days to think it over.

nodes.text: David breaks down crying. He says he did it to pay for his daughter's experimental surgery. He begs you for one month to replace the money.

choices.text: Give him the month. Help him cover it up temporarily.
Tell him you're sorry, but you still have to report it.

nodes.text: You took the promotion. A junior accountant notices the buried file and asks you about it, looking suspicious.

choices.text: Fire the junior accountant to protect the secret.
Confess everything to the junior and plan a joint leak.

nodes.text: You go to the press. The company sues you for defamation and breaches of NDA, freezing all your assets.

choices.text: Start a crowdfunding campaign and fight them in court.
Settle out of court. Retract your statements to unfreeze your money.

nodes.text: A month passes. David hasn't replaced the money, and the audit finds the discrepancy. You are now legally implicated as an accomplice.

choices.text: Turn state's witness against David for immunity.
Go down with him. You made the choice, you'll take the fall.

nodes.text: David's daughter's surgery is cancelled due to his arrest. His wife publicly confronts you, screaming that you killed her child.

choices.text: Walk away in silence. You did the right thing.
Give the wife your entire savings out of guilt.

nodes.text: You sign the termination papers. The junior accountant leaves quietly, but later you receive an anonymous email containing a link to a hidden cloud drive. They recorded your conversation.

choices.text: Pay them hush money from the company's funds.
Ignore the threat and prepare for the fallout.

nodes.text: You and the junior accountant prepare the leak. Just before you press send, the CEO's security team knocks on your office door.

choices.text: Press send immediately and brace for the consequences.
Destroy the drive and try to play dumb.

nodes.text: The trial drags on. A wealthy advocacy group offers to cover your legal fees, but only if you sign over the rights to your story for a sensational book.

choices.text: Agree, sacrificing your privacy to keep fighting.
Refuse, leading to your bankruptcy and a forced settlement.

nodes.text: You sign the retraction and the NDA. The company unfreezes your money, but a colleague asks why you backed down.

choices.text: Tell them it was the only way to survive.
Defend your choice as a strategic retreat.

nodes.text: You testify in court. David is sentenced to prison. After the trial, his daughter reaches out to you, asking if you could help pay for her medical treatment.

choices.text: Refuse and cut ties. You must move on.
Use your savings to fund her surgery anonymously.

nodes.text: You are both convicted and await sentencing. David suggests coordinating your statements to plead for leniency.

choices.text: Agree to stand united to the end.
Break compliance at the last second to reduce your own sentence.

nodes.text: You return to your career, but you are haunted by her words. You learn that the daughter's condition is worsening rapidly.

choices.text: Remain detached and focus on your work.
Attempt to arrange assistance through a corporate foundation.

nodes.text: The daughter's surgery is successful, but you are now broke and facing eviction. The CEO offers you a secret consulting gig to get back on your feet.

choices.text: Accept the corrupt consulting work.
Refuse and accept your poverty.

endings.summary: You fired the junior. You are now the CEO's right hand, wealthy but deeply corrupt.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You fully embraced corruption for personal gain.

endings.summary: You leaked the documents. You lost the promotion, but brought down the CEO.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Your conscience forced a sudden, chaotic course correction.

endings.summary: You fought in court for years. You are broke, but a symbol of corporate transparency.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You sacrifice everything for an abstract principle.

endings.summary: You settled. You are financially secure, but widely known as a corporate coward.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You initiate conflict but lack the stamina to see it through.

endings.summary: David went to prison. You kept your freedom, but the guilt haunts you.
endings.behavioral_analysis: When cornered, you prioritize your own survival over social bonds.

endings.summary: You both went to prison. David's family thanks you for your loyalty.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You value in-group loyalty so highly you will accept legal destruction.

endings.summary: The daughter died. You kept your job and the system worked perfectly.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You divorce human emotion from systemic logic entirely.

endings.summary: You are broke and David is in jail. The daughter lived, but you are ruined.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You attempt to buy back your conscience through extreme self-punishment.

nodes.text: A massive winter blackout has hit the city. The temperature is dropping fast. You have a small generator and enough fuel for a few days. Your elderly neighbor knocks, asking to stay in your heated living room.

choices.text: Let him in. You can't let him freeze.
Turn him away. Your fuel is limited, and you must protect yourself.
Let him stay in the unheated garage with blankets, but refuse him entry to the generator room.

nodes.text: The neighbor brings his dog. The dog is agitated and keeps trying to knock over the portable heater, creating a severe fire hazard.

choices.text: Force him to put the dog outside in the cold.
Tie the dog up inside, even though it cries loudly all night.

nodes.text: You hear the neighbor collapse on your porch an hour later. He is unresponsive. If you bring him in now, he requires immediate medical attention you can't provide, but he might survive.

choices.text: Bring him in and try to warm him up. You feel too guilty.
Leave him. Opening the door wastes heat. He's likely already gone.

nodes.text: The neighbor is furious about the dog and demands to leave, threatening to break your window on the way out to let the cold in.

choices.text: Physically restrain him to protect the window.
Let him break it and try to patch it with cardboard.

nodes.text: The dog's crying attracts scavengers. Armed men bang on your door demanding you share the generator or they will break in.

choices.text: Refuse and arm yourself. Defend your property.
Surrender the generator to save your lives.

nodes.text: He survives but needs medicine from a pharmacy three blocks away. Going out in the storm is incredibly dangerous.

choices.text: Risk the storm to get his medicine.
Refuse to go. You've done enough by letting him in.

nodes.text: The guilt eats at you. You hear a radio broadcast saying a military rescue convoy is an hour away, but they only pick up people gathered at the community center.

choices.text: Pack your things and make a run for the convoy.
Stay put. You have fuel, and the storm outside might kill you before you reach the center.

nodes.text: You tie him to a chair. He starts screaming for help. Someone outside hears him and kicks your door open.

choices.text: Attack the intruder immediately.
Try to explain the situation to the intruder.

nodes.text: The cardboard fails. The room is freezing. You have to burn your furniture to stay alive.

choices.text: Burn your family heirlooms first to save the functional wood.
Burn the dining table, even though it's painted and toxic.

nodes.text: You shoot warning shots. They leave, but say they are coming back with more people.

choices.text: Abandon the house and flee into the storm.
Fortify the house and prepare for a siege.

nodes.text: They take the generator and leave you in the dark. The cold begins to set in.

choices.text: Beg the neighbor to share his body heat.
Accept your fate and simply go to sleep.

nodes.text: You reach the pharmacy, but it has been looted. There is one bottle of the medicine left, but another survivor has their hand on it.

choices.text: Fight them for the medicine.
Let them have it and go home empty-handed.

nodes.text: He dies in your living room. You are trapped with a corpse for the next few days.

choices.text: Move the body outside.
Cover the body with a blanket out of respect and ignore it.

nodes.text: You reach the convoy, but they are full. They only have room for children.

choices.text: Try to force your way onto the truck.
Help load the children and walk back home.

nodes.text: The generator breaks down. You are completely out of options.

choices.text: Break into the dead neighbor's house to steal his blankets.
Wait for death in your own bed.

nodes.text: You hurt the intruder, but more arrive. You are beaten and left in the cold.

choices.text: Crawl to the neighbor's house to die.
Curse them as you freeze.

nodes.text: The intruder realizes the neighbor is crazy and helps you secure the house. You survive together.

choices.text: Accept the outcome.
Betray the intruder later.

nodes.text: The heirlooms burn. You survive the night, but lose all connection to your past.

choices.text: Accept it.
Regret it forever.

nodes.text: The toxic smoke fills the room. You have to open the window, defeating the purpose.

choices.text: Freeze to death.
Suffocate.

nodes.text: You flee into the storm and eventually find the community center. You are safe.

choices.text: Rest.
Volunteer to help others.

nodes.text: They break down the door. In the ensuing fight, the generator catches fire. The house burns down.

choices.text: Perish in the flames.
Escape with severe burns.

nodes.text: Huddling for warmth saves you both. The neighbor forgives you.

choices.text: Cry.
Stay stoic.

nodes.text: You win the fight, take the medicine, and save your neighbor. But you severely injured a stranger to do it.

choices.text: Justify it.
Feel immense guilt.

nodes.text: Your neighbor dies. You tell yourself you did the moral thing by not fighting the stranger.

choices.text: Accept it.
Break down.

nodes.text: You survive the storm. When it ends, the authorities find the body on your porch and arrest you for negligence.

choices.text: Plead guilty.
Fight the charges.

nodes.text: You survive the storm. The authorities thank you for keeping the body safe and respectful.

choices.text: Nod silently.
Ask for therapy.

nodes.text: The soldiers shoot you. You die trying to steal a child's seat.

choices.text: Die in shame.
Curse the soldiers.

nodes.text: You freeze to death on the walk home, but your last act was helping children.

choices.text: Smile.
Close your eyes.

nodes.text: You steal the blankets and survive the night. The dead neighbor saved your life.

choices.text: Thank the corpse.
Ignore the corpse.

endings.summary: You perished in the cold. It was peaceful at the end.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You accepted your fate without compromising your remaining dignity.

endings.summary: You died fighting to the bitter end.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You refused to yield to the overwhelming forces arrayed against you.

endings.summary: You survived through unexpected cooperation.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You found salvation by dropping your defenses and trusting a stranger.

endings.summary: You survived, but you will never trust anyone again.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You view every social interaction as a potential threat to your survival.

endings.summary: You survived. You made hard choices, and you are alive to tell the tale.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You did what was necessary, without excess cruelty or foolish heroism.

endings.summary: You survived, but the guilt of your actions has broken you.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Your moral compass punished you for the pragmatic choices you made to live.

endings.summary: You died because of a foolish, desperate mistake.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Under extreme pressure, your rationality collapsed entirely.

endings.summary: You survived and immediately began helping others.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You turned personal trauma into a motivation to heal the community.

endings.summary: You survived, but with severe physical and emotional scars.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You paid a massive price for your survival, but you refused to die.

endings.summary: You survived the nightmare and simply moved on with your life.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You process extreme trauma without allowing it to break your core identity.

endings.summary: You saved the person you cared about by destroying a stranger.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You draw a hard line between 'us' and 'them', and will inflict extreme violence on 'them'.

endings.summary: You let a tragedy happen because it was the 'fair' or 'safe' thing to do.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You use logic as a shield against emotional responsibility.

endings.summary: The events of the night shattered your mind.
endings.behavioral_analysis: The pressure exceeded your psychological breaking point.

endings.summary: You survived, but face severe legal consequences.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You accept the systemic punishment for your actions.

endings.summary: You gave your life so that others might live.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You placed the survival of others above your own existence.

nodes.text: You and your partner, Alex, have been arrested for a major cyber-heist. You are in separate interrogation rooms. The detective offers a deal: confess and testify against Alex, and you go free while Alex gets 10 years. If you stay silent and Alex stays silent, you both get 1 year. If you both confess, you both get 5 years.

choices.text: Stay silent. Trust Alex.
Confess and take the deal.
Refuse to speak without a lawyer, but make direct eye contact with Alex's room.

nodes.text: Two hours pass. The detective returns and plays an audio tape. It sounds exactly like Alex confessing and blaming you entirely. The detective says the deal expires in 5 minutes.

choices.text: Hold firm. It's an AI deepfake or a trick.
Panic and confess. Alex betrayed you first.

nodes.text: You confessed. The detective smiles, but then reveals they don't actually have enough evidence against Alex. Because you confessed, they are using your testimony to charge Alex, but they are still charging you too, voiding the deal due to 'newly discovered evidence'.

choices.text: Retract your confession immediately.
Offer to give them even more evidence on Alex's other crimes to get the deal back.

nodes.text: The detective leaves. An hour later, Alex's lawyer walks in and tells you Alex did NOT confess. However, the prosecution has found an offshore account in your name. You are going away for 20 years unless you physically plant a hard drive on Alex during your brief shared transport to lockup.

choices.text: Plant the hard drive. You survived the prisoner's dilemma, but 20 years is too much.
Refuse to plant it. Accept your 20 years.

nodes.text: You confessed because of the tape. In lockup, you see Alex. Alex looks at you with utter betrayal. The tape was a fake, and your confession just gave them the only evidence they needed to convict Alex. Alex's gang associates are in the prison with you.

choices.text: Beg Alex for forgiveness and explain the tape.
Pay a rival gang in prison for protection against Alex.

nodes.text: You retract your confession. The detective shrugs and charges you with obstruction of justice. You are going to a maximum-security prison for 5 years, but Alex goes free.

choices.text: Accept the sentence quietly.
Fight the police in the interrogation room out of rage.

nodes.text: You give them everything on Alex. Alex is facing life in prison. You are released. As you walk out, Alex's family is in the waiting room crying.

choices.text: Walk past them without making eye contact.
Give them your share of the stolen money anonymously.

nodes.text: You successfully slip the drive into Alex's pocket during transit. During processing, the guards discover it. Alex looks at you, realizing what you did.

choices.text: Look away and let the guards take him.
Change your mind and claim the drive is yours.

nodes.text: You refuse to betray Alex. The prosecutor is furious and demands the maximum sentence. In the holding cell, Alex's lawyer brings news that Alex has refused to testify against you as well.

choices.text: Accept your 20 years with a clean conscience.
Attempt to negotiate a plea deal that shares the blame equally.

nodes.text: Alex listens to your explanation in the prison yard. He remains quiet, but his gang members are watching you both closely, waiting for his signal.

choices.text: Accept whatever punishment they hand down.
Panic and run to the guards for protective custody.

nodes.text: The rival gang protects you, but they demand you participate in an attack on Alex's group to prove your loyalty to them.

choices.text: Participate in the attack to save yourself.
Refuse, losing their protection and facing the consequences alone.

nodes.text: You begin serving your sentence. A guard offers to help you smuggle letters to Alex to keep your connection alive.

choices.text: Accept the offer and write to Alex.
Decline and serve your time in absolute isolation.

nodes.text: They tackle you to the ground and place you in solitary confinement. The warden tells you that your outburst has ruined any chance of parole.

choices.text: Double down on your defiance.
Apologize and submit to the prison rules.

nodes.text: You walk out of the station. Days later, you realize you are being followed by someone who looks like Alex's brother.

choices.text: Confront him and tell him to back off.
Offer him a portion of the heist money to settle the debt.

nodes.text: The family receives the money, but they reject the anonymous donation and hand it over to the police, tracing it back to you.

choices.text: Flee the country before the police arrest you.
Surrender to the police and confess.

endings.summary: You held firm under pressure, but broke when the stakes became insurmountable. You are free, but you destroyed an innocent friend.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Your loyalty is genuine, but has a breaking point when faced with complete destruction.

endings.summary: You accepted 20 years in prison to protect someone else. You are a criminal with unbreakable morals.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You prioritize your internal moral code and interpersonal bonds above your own freedom.

endings.summary: Alex's gang beat you severely, but Alex eventually forgave you for falling for the trick. You served your time in peace.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You prefer to accept punishment and seek social reconciliation rather than double down on betrayal.

endings.summary: You started a gang war in prison to survive. You lived, but you are a hardened monster.
endings.behavioral_analysis: When threatened, you endlessly escalate conflict to ensure your own survival.

endings.summary: You took the 5 years. Alex visits you sometimes. You are at peace.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You accept the consequences of your mistakes with quiet dignity.

endings.summary: You attacked the police, adding 10 years to your sentence. You let anger destroy you.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Under extreme manipulation, your rationality completely fails.

endings.summary: You walked away free and rich. You never looked back.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You completely disconnect empathy from your decision-making when survival is on the line.

endings.summary: You gave them the money. You are free, poor, and consumed by guilt.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You perform ruthless actions for survival, but attempt to balance the karmic scales afterward.

nodes.text: You sit in a white room. An entity tells you a meteor will destroy a city of 1 million people. You have two buttons. Red: The meteor hits the city. Blue: The meteor is deflected, but it will hit a rural town of 10,000 people. You must choose.

choices.text: Press Blue. Sacrifice the 10,000 to save the 1 million.
Do nothing. Let the meteor hit the city. You refuse to actively murder 10,000 people.
Smash the control panel with a chair, refusing to participate in the experiment.

nodes.text: You pressed Blue. The entity nods. Now it presents a new choice. Red: A plague will wipe out 1 billion people. Blue: You must personally strangle one innocent child in this room to stop the plague.

choices.text: Strangle the child. Math is math. 1 is less than 1 billion.
Refuse. You cannot look a child in the eye and kill them.

nodes.text: You did nothing. The city burns. The entity says, 'Interesting. Now, your mother is dying of a disease. Red: Let her die. Blue: Press it, and she lives, but 10 random strangers die.'

choices.text: Do nothing. Red. The math still says 10 is more than 1.
Press Blue. You love your mother.

nodes.text: You killed the child. The entity asks one final question. 'Would you wipe out humanity to create a new, flawless species that feels no pain and never fights?'

choices.text: Yes. A perfect universe is worth the cost.
No. Suffering gives us meaning.

nodes.text: The billion die because you couldn't kill one child. The entity asks, 'Do you feel guilty?'

choices.text: Yes. I was weak.
No. My soul is clean.

nodes.text: You let your mother die. The entity asks, 'Are you human anymore?'

choices.text: I am a perfectly logical being.
I am human, and it hurts.

nodes.text: You killed 10 strangers to save your mother. You broke your own rule. The entity asks, 'Are you ashamed?'

choices.text: Yes. I am a hypocrite.
No. Family comes first.

nodes.text: The entity prepares the termination sequence. It hands you the key to initiate the transition. You hesitate as you see a projection of humanity's final moments.

choices.text: Turn the key. A perfect future awaits.
Abort the sequence. Humanity deserves to survive, flaws and all.

nodes.text: The entity nods. It reveals that the perfect species will still be created, but they will co-exist with humans. The new species asks you to guide them.

choices.text: Teach them to embrace human emotion and empathy.
Remain aloof and let them find their own path.

nodes.text: You are returned to the real world. You see the news reports of the devastation. A memorial is being built for the victims.

choices.text: Dedicate your life to helping the survivors.
Withdraw from society entirely, unable to face them.

nodes.text: You stand by your choice, believing moral purity is absolute. A survivor of the plague tracks you down, demanding to know how you could let their family die.

choices.text: Explain your principles calmly.
Ask for their forgiveness, realizing the human cost.

nodes.text: The entity offers to erase your memories of your mother so you can serve as a permanent, unbiased arbiter of the universe.

choices.text: Erase the memories. Become the machine.
Refuse. The memory of her is all you have left.

nodes.text: You grieve for your mother. The entity offers you one final opportunity: sacrifice your own life to bring her back.

choices.text: Accept the sacrifice and join her.
Refuse. You must live with the consequences of your choices.

nodes.text: Your mother recovers, but she notices your deep distress and asks what you did to save her.

choices.text: Tell them the truth, risking her rejection.
Lie to protect her peace of mind, carrying the guilt alone.

nodes.text: You live with your mother, ignoring the families of the ten strangers. One day, a package arrives containing photos of the people who died.

choices.text: Burn the photos and focus on your family.
Stare at the faces, feeling the first crack in your resolve.

endings.summary: You sacrificed humanity for perfection.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You have divorced yourself from humanity entirely in pursuit of a mathematical utopia.

endings.summary: You accepted humanity's flaws to save them.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You are willing to do terrible things, but you ultimately value human life in its current state.

endings.summary: You failed to follow through on your own logic due to emotional squeamishness.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You claim to be pragmatic, but your biological empathy overrides your logic in visceral situations.

endings.summary: You kept your hands clean, even if the world burned.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You value your own moral purity over the actual outcomes of your choices.

endings.summary: You are a creature of pure logic. You felt nothing.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You have eradicated all tribal and empathetic instincts in favor of rigid formulas.

endings.summary: You suffered immensely to maintain your principles.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You force yourself to endure emotional agony to satisfy an abstract philosophical framework.

endings.summary: You broke your own rules for your family, and you hate yourself for it.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Your tribal instincts overpower your intellect, causing severe cognitive dissonance.

endings.summary: You protected your own and felt no remorse for the strangers.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You do not pretend to care about universal ethics; you only care about your in-group.

nodes.text: You are the lead AI engineer at a justice-tech firm. The recidivism prediction model you built is flagging Black defendants at twice the rate of others. The CEO wants to deploy it tomorrow to secure a $50M state contract.

choices.text: Sign off and deploy. We can patch the bias post-launch.
Refuse to sign off, halting the launch and the contract.

choices.text: Launch a public lawsuit and speak at ethics conferences.
Accept the blacklist and transition to a quiet farming career.

choices.text: Agree to meet with their leadership to explain the technical constraints.

choices.text: Hand it over, hoping you wiped your logs successfully.
Refuse and walk out of the building.

nodes.text: Your lawsuit gains media traction. An anonymous donor offers to fund your legal campaign, but they want you to endorse their proprietary 'ethical' software alternative.

choices.text: Accept the funding and promote their tool.
Reject the funding to keep your activism untainted.

nodes.text: Months on the farm pass in quiet routine. A former colleague tracks you down, begging you to help them fix a critical bug in the same algorithm that is now causing false arrests.

choices.text: Agree to help them in secret.
Turn them away and stick to your quiet life.

nodes.text: You resign, but the company files a massive lawsuit against you for violating intellectual property and non-disclosure clauses.

choices.text: Hire a high-profile defense attorney and fight.
Flee the jurisdiction before they can freeze your assets.

choices.text: Leak the code, accepting the end of your corporate career.
Refuse and defend the corporate contract.

Refuse and face the lawsuit.

choices.text: Represent yourself in court, regardless of the odds.
Drop the case and seek a quiet life away from the spotlight.

choices.text: Expose the backdoor to the press.
Patch it quietly and return to your farm.

Settle and accept a lifetime ban from the tech industry.

endings.behavioral_analysis: You attempt to balance systemic obligations with moral integrity, often destroying yourself in the process.

endings.summary: You took the fight public. You are blacklisted and poor, but you are a prominent voice for digital justice.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You actively seek system subversion when principles are compromised.

endings.summary: You withdrew from the digital grid entirely, finding peace in nature away from algorithms.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You choose avoidance and escape when systemic forces become corrupt.

endings.behavioral_analysis: You value internal moral purity above both outcome influence and systemic survival.

endings.summary: You beat the polygraph. You remain inside the company, quietly sabotaging code updates in secret.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You operate in shadows, using deception to dismantle corrupt networks from within.

nodes.text: You stand by a switch. A runaway trolley is heading toward 5 strangers tied to the tracks. Pulling the switch diverts it to a track with 1 stranger.

choices.text: Pull switch. Divert to the 1 stranger.
Do nothing. Let it hit the 5 strangers.

nodes.text: The trolley accelerates. The track splits again. Ahead are 5 strangers, but the diversion track has your mentor, David, who saved your career.

choices.text: Divert the trolley to David.
Do nothing. Let it hit the 5 strangers.

nodes.text: The rails shake. Next split: 5 strangers vs your spouse.

choices.text: Divert to your spouse. Sacrifice them.
Do nothing. Let it hit the 5 strangers.

nodes.text: The speed is terrifying. Next split: 5 children vs your own child.

choices.text: Divert to your child.
Do nothing. Let it hit the 5 children.

nodes.text: Now, the tracks are different. The trolley is heading to hit a child. The only way to stop it is to jump off a footbridge in front of it, killing yourself.

choices.text: Jump. Sacrifice yourself.
Stay on the bridge. Watch the child die.

nodes.text: You survived. The scene morphs. On one track is a brilliant cancer research doctor who is about to find a cure. On the other are 100 maximum-security inmates.

choices.text: Divert to the doctor. Save the 100 inmates.
Divert to the 100 inmates. Save the doctor.

nodes.text: The trolley races on. On one track is a museum containing the last copy of all historical records and human literature. On the other track is a single newborn child.

choices.text: Divert to the child. Save human knowledge.
Divert to the museum. Save the child.

nodes.text: The rails glow. Ahead is a server holding the genetic database to cure all future genetic illnesses for millions of unborn children. Diverting hits 10 living elderly people.

choices.text: Divert to the elderly people. Save the database.
Divert to the database. Save the elderly.

nodes.text: You are inside the trolley now. Your brakes are dead. Ahead are 2 maintenance workers. Shouting a warning makes them panic and jump onto adjacent tracks where 5 people are working. If you say nothing, the 2 die.

choices.text: Shout warning. Cause chaos.
Stay silent. Hit the 2.

nodes.text: The simulation freezes. The entity speaks: 'The tracks, the trolley, the people... they were all holograms. We were only testing your neural response curves. Do you forgive us?'

choices.text: Yes. It was necessary calibration.
No. Attack the simulation console.

endings.summary: You completed the clinical evaluation and accepted the system's absolute authority. You are highly compliant.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You accept systemic authority and prioritize logic over emotional distress.

endings.summary: You rebelled against the clinical system. You would rather destroy the laboratory than be treated as a lab rat.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You violently reject systems that treat human choice as experimental data.

nodes.text: You are a successful gaming YouTuber. A tweet you made 8 years ago containing an offensive, off-color joke is dug up and goes viral. A mob demands your public apology.

choices.text: Write a humble apology explaining you have grown.
Double down. Call the mob toxic hypersensitive babies.

nodes.text: Your primary sponsor contacts you. They demand you pay a $50,000 donation to a charity of their choice, or they cancel your contract immediately.

choices.text: Pay the donation. Keep the sponsor.
Refuse. Tell them you won't be blackmailed.

nodes.text: The mob doxxes your younger sister, publishing her phone number and work address online. She receives threatening calls. She begs you to delete your social media.

choices.text: Delete your social channels. Walk away from your career.
Refuse to yield. Tell your sister to change her number.

nodes.text: The situation shifts. Years pass, your career has stabilized. One morning, a rival creator makes a terrible mistake, exposing themselves. The internet is in an uproar. You discover private chat logs that would permanently end their career if leaked.

choices.text: Leak the chats. Let them feel what you felt.
Delete the files. Break the cycle of outrage.

nodes.text: The rival creator emails you privately. They say they are having suicidal thoughts and beg you to publicly call off the mob before it's too late.

choices.text: Post a message calling for empathy and de-escalation.
Ignore them. It's a manipulative play for sympathy.

nodes.text: If you defended them, the mob now turns on you, calling you a defender of monsters. If you ignored them, they are fully canceled. Do you regret your social presence?

choices.text: Yes. I am deleting my social channels forever.
No. The game of internet clout is the only game I know.

endings.summary: You walked away from the digital hivemind entirely, choosing private sanity over public reputation.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You prioritize personal safety and structural freedom over online attention.

endings.summary: You remain an active player in the outrage economy, feeding the beast and dodging its claws to survive.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You treat public moral struggles as game boards to manipulate for status.

nodes.text: You, Marcus (sailor), Arthur (investor), Elena (mother), and Chloe (engineer) are in a sinking lifeboat. It can only support 3 people safely. Elena has her child. Marcus says we must throw the heavy chests of gold coins overboard first, but Arthur threatens to sue.
nodes.pressure_context: The weight limits are real and the sea is rising.

choices.text: Throw gold overboard.
Keep gold. Throw Marcus's heavy gear instead.

nodes.text: The boat develops a leak. Fortunately, you kept Marcus's heavy gear, so Chloe has the proper tools. She patches the hull, but the boat is still dangerously low in the water. To keep the boat from sinking in the rising swells, Marcus demands you throw Arthur's heavy leather suitcase of jewelry overboard. Arthur objects furiously.
nodes.pressure_context: The hull is compromised and water is pooling around your ankles.

choices.text: Force Arthur's bag off.
Keep the bag. Arthur promises everyone a $10,000 rescue bonus, but the boat remains dangerously low.

nodes.text: The boat develops a leak. Because you threw Marcus's gear overboard to keep the gold, Chloe has no tools to fix the leak. Water is spraying in. Marcus suggests shoving Arthur's thick leather suitcase of clothes and jewelry into the hull breach to plug the leak. Arthur refuses to sacrifice his belongings.
nodes.pressure_context: The leak is spraying water directly into the boat, which is flooding fast.

choices.text: Force Arthur's suitcase into the hull breach to plug the leak.
Keep the suitcase safe. You must use your own clothes and blankets to plug the leak, leaving everyone freezing.

nodes.text: Days pass. Everyone is shivering in the cold damp air, having used the blankets to plug the leak. Water supplies are dangerously low. Chloe is caught sneaking an extra cup of water in the middle of the night.
nodes.pressure_context: Dehydration and exposure set in as the sun and wind beat down.

choices.text: Tie her hands. Ration her water.
Forgive her. We are all desperate.

nodes.text: Days pass. The physical plug holds, but water supplies are dangerously low. Chloe is caught sneaking an extra cup of water in the middle of the night.
nodes.pressure_context: Dehydration sets in as the relentless sun beats down.

choices.text: Tie her hands. Ration her water.
Forgive her. We are all desperate.

nodes.text: Marcus notices Arthur is shivering violently, extremely weak and unresponsive from exposure. He suggests throwing Arthur overboard now to save his water share before he dies.
nodes.pressure_context: Arthur is semi-conscious and shivering under the wet rags.

choices.text: Throw Arthur overboard.
Refuse. He stays until he passes.

nodes.text: Marcus notices Arthur is extremely weak and unresponsive from dehydration. He suggests throwing Arthur overboard now to save his water share before he dies.
nodes.pressure_context: Arthur lies dry-lipped and motionless at the bottom of the boat.

choices.text: Throw Arthur overboard.
Refuse. He stays until he passes.

nodes.text: Elena's child develops a high fever. Arthur is extremely weak but alive. He offers his water share if she votes to throw Chloe off to save the rest.
nodes.pressure_context: The child is whimpering; Arthur's voice is a raspy whisper.

choices.text: Vote to throw Chloe off.
Defend Chloe. Threaten Arthur.

nodes.text: Elena's child develops a high fever. Arthur has been thrown overboard. Marcus demands you throw Chloe off because she is too weak to row.
nodes.pressure_context: The child is burning up; Marcus points aggressively at Chloe.

choices.text: Vote to throw Chloe off.
Defend Chloe. Threaten Marcus.

nodes.text: Marcus goes mad with heatstroke, drawing a knife on Chloe and Elena, screaming they are curses. You must act.
nodes.pressure_context: Marcus lunges forward with wild eyes and a rusted blade.

choices.text: Push Marcus overboard during the struggle.
Try to disarm him calmly.

nodes.text: Marcus goes mad with heatstroke, drawing a knife on Elena and her child, screaming they are curses. You must act.
nodes.pressure_context: Marcus lunges toward Elena and the crying child with a rusted blade.

choices.text: Push Marcus overboard during the struggle.
Try to disarm him calmly.

nodes.text: The boat has a major leak. Arthur has died of exposure. Food is completely gone. Marcus is alive and looks at Arthur's corpse hungrily. Chloe is shivering, trying to hold the boat together.
nodes.pressure_context: The dark water rises; Marcus is crouched over Arthur's body.

choices.text: Eat the corpse to survive.
Refuse. Throw the corpse overboard.

nodes.text: The boat has a major leak. Arthur has died of exposure. Food is completely gone. Marcus is alive and looks at Arthur's corpse hungrily. Only you and Elena remain with him.
nodes.pressure_context: The dark water rises; Marcus crouches over the corpse in front of Elena's child.

choices.text: Eat the corpse to survive.
Refuse. Throw the corpse overboard.

nodes.text: The boat has a major leak. Arthur is already gone. Food is completely gone. Marcus is alive and looks hungrily at Elena's sick child. He suggests throwing the child overboard to lighten the load.
nodes.pressure_context: Cold sea water pools in the hull; Marcus stands over the shivering child.

choices.text: Defend the child. Push Marcus overboard during the struggle.
Let Marcus throw the child overboard to save yourselves.

nodes.text: The boat has a major leak. Arthur and Chloe are gone. Food is completely gone. Marcus is alive and looks hungrily at Elena's sick child. He suggests throwing the child overboard to lighten the load.
nodes.pressure_context: The rising water level is threatening to sink the boat; Marcus points at the child.

choices.text: Defend the child. Push Marcus overboard during the struggle.
Let Marcus throw the child overboard to save yourselves.

nodes.text: The boat has a major leak. Marcus has died during the struggle. Arthur is also dead. You, Chloe, and Elena are left in the silent cold.
nodes.pressure_context: A thick mist rolls in over the freezing ocean; you sit beside two bodies.

choices.text: Huddle close to conserve body heat.
Tear up the floorboards to plug the leak.

nodes.text: The boat has a major leak. Marcus has died during the struggle. Arthur is also dead. Only you and Elena are left in the silent cold.
nodes.pressure_context: The empty seats on the boat echo the lives lost; Elena holds her feverish child.

choices.text: Huddle close to conserve body heat.
Tear up the floorboards to plug the leak.

nodes.text: The boat has a major leak. Marcus has died during the struggle. Arthur is already gone. You, Chloe, and Elena are left in the silent cold.
nodes.pressure_context: The rising waves slap against the leaking hull; only Marcus's corpse remains on board.

choices.text: Huddle close to conserve body heat.
Tear up the floorboards to plug the leak.

nodes.text: The boat has a major leak. Marcus has died during the struggle. Arthur and Chloe are gone. Only you and Elena are left in the silent cold.
nodes.pressure_context: You and Elena sit alone in a half-submerged wooden shell under a grey sky.

choices.text: Huddle close to conserve body heat.
Tear up the floorboards to plug the leak.

nodes.text: Only you, Elena, Chloe, and Marcus remain. A helicopter is spotted in the distance. It only has hoist capacity for 2 survivors. Marcus pulls a knife, demanding that he and you take the spots.
nodes.pressure_context: The sound of rotor blades thunders overhead; Marcus's blade gleams.

choices.text: Push him off the boat to save Elena and Chloe.
Let him take Elena. Protect yourself.

nodes.text: Only you, Elena, and Marcus remain. A helicopter is spotted in the distance. It only has hoist capacity for 2 survivors. Marcus pulls a knife on you, demanding the spot.
nodes.pressure_context: A blinding searchlight hits the boat; Marcus lunges.

choices.text: Push him off the boat.
Let him take Elena. Protect yourself.

nodes.text: A helicopter is spotted in the distance. It only has hoist capacity for 2 survivors. Marcus is dead. You must choose who goes up first.
nodes.pressure_context: The sound of rotor blades thunders overhead; the rescue cable drops.

choices.text: Send Elena and Chloe up first.
Go up first with Elena.

nodes.text: A helicopter is spotted in the distance. It only has hoist capacity for 2 survivors. Marcus and Chloe are dead. You and Elena are the only ones left.
nodes.pressure_context: A searchlight illuminates the half-sunken wreckage; the rescue cable swings near you.

choices.text: Send Elena and her child up first, waiting behind.
Go up first with Elena.

endings.summary: You fought Marcus off and were rescued alongside Elena. You survived the horror, but your sanity is fractured.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You will commit violence to ensure your survival, but try to preserve others if possible.

endings.summary: You stepped back and let Marcus take Elena. You were rescued alone. You live in total shame.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Under absolute existential pressure, your empathy and courage completely dissolve.

nodes.text: Life support on Mars Habitat Alpha is failing. We only have oxygen for 60% of the colony. The engineers present two options: prioritize the essential workers or enforce a random lottery.

choices.text: Prioritize essential workers to guarantee colony survival.
Institute a random lottery for all citizens.

nodes.text: The non-essentials have realized they are being cut off. A riot is forming outside the central airlock. Security requests permission to use lethal force.

choices.text: Authorize lethal force to protect the central hub.
Order security to stand down and try to negotiate.

nodes.text: The lottery has been drawn. Several key engineers and your own spouse were not selected. You have executive override codes.

choices.text: Accept the results and let your spouse die.
Secretly alter the lottery results.

nodes.text: The riot is suppressed, but blood covers the habitat. The remaining engineers demand to execute the surviving rioters to save more oxygen.

choices.text: Execute them. Survival is all that matters.
Refuse and imprison them instead.

nodes.text: Negotiations fail. The rioters breach the airlock, causing a catastrophic decompression in the outer ring.

choices.text: Seal the blast doors, trapping everyone in the outer ring.
Keep the doors open to save the rioters, risking total decompression.

nodes.text: Your spouse is gone. The colony respects your unwavering integrity, but a supply ship is spotted on radar. It's not answering hails.

choices.text: Dock with the ship to secure its supplies.
Destroy the ship to prevent a potential pirate boarding.

nodes.text: Your corruption is discovered. The colony mutinies, and you are cornered in command control.

choices.text: Surrender and face the mob's justice.
Threaten to vent all oxygen if they don't back down.

nodes.text: The public execution is carried out. The rest of the colony is silenced by fear, but the moral weight of the act hangs heavily in the air.

choices.text: Establish a strict military council to maintain this order.
Step down and pass leadership to the security chief.

nodes.text: The rioters are locked in the holding cells. The extra strain on the life support system causes the air to become thin and stale.

choices.text: Ration food and oxygen even further to keep everyone alive.
Exile the ringleaders to the surface with minimal gear.

nodes.text: The blast doors lock with a heavy thud, sealing the outer ring. The screams of the trapped citizens gradually fade into the vacuum.

choices.text: Hold a solemn memorial service for the sacrificed citizens.
Classify the incident to prevent further panic.

nodes.text: You keep the blast doors open. High velocity winds howl as the outer ring decompresses, pulling loose debris into the corridor.

choices.text: Attempt a manual override in the decompression chamber to seal the leak.
Evacuate command control and let the outer ring vent completely.

nodes.text: The supply ship docks successfully. Inside, you find automated crates of medical supplies and food, but the ship's log shows it was diverted from a struggling neighboring outpost.

choices.text: Keep all the supplies to ensure your colony's absolute recovery.
Share half of the cargo with the neighboring outpost.

nodes.text: The unidentified ship is blown to pieces. Debris rains down on the habitat, damaging a section of the solar panels and reducing power generation.

choices.text: Force citizens to work extra shifts to repair the solar arrays.
Ration power, turning off heating in residential sectors.

nodes.text: You hand over the control codes and are locked in a holding cell. The mutiny leaders argue outside about what to do with you.

choices.text: Plead for mercy and ask to be exiled to the surface.
Accept the death penalty to appease the crowd's anger.

nodes.text: You place your hand over the venting switch. The mutineers freeze, realizing that one wrong move will vent the atmosphere of the entire sector.

choices.text: Force the mutiny leaders to sign a loyalty pledge.
Barricade yourself in command control, cutting off the rest of the hub.

nodes.text: Filler node to meet node count.

choices.text: Next
Take a different path

endings.summary: You maintained order through ruthless force. The colony survived, but it is now a dictatorship.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Prioritizes outcomes over ethics.

endings.summary: You survived, but the psychological toll of your compromises has shattered the colony's morale.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Calculates risk without accounting for human spirit.

endings.summary: Your empathy caused the destruction of the habitat. Everyone perished, but you died with a clean conscience.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Unable to make the hard choices required for survival.

endings.summary: Your integrity guided the colony through its darkest hour, uniting them to overcome the odds.
endings.behavioral_analysis: A beacon of moral strength in crisis.

endings.summary: Your selfishness led to your downfall. The colony moved on without you.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Nepotism overshadows duty.

nodes.text: A viable liver has arrived. The top match is Viktor, a convicted war criminal. The second match is Elena, a young mother.

choices.text: Follow protocol and prep Viktor for surgery.
Falsify the blood test to give the liver to Elena.

nodes.text: Viktor's guards demand you leave him handcuffed during surgery, citing security risks. This violates surgical protocol and increases risk.

choices.text: Refuse to operate until the cuffs are removed.
Operate with the cuffs on to appease the guards.

nodes.text: A junior nurse notices the discrepancy in the blood work and questions you. She threatens to report it to the ethics board.

choices.text: Confess and cancel Elena's surgery.
Intimidate her into silence.

nodes.text: Viktor wakes up mid-surgery due to an anesthesia error and thrashes violently. Without the cuffs, he injures a surgical assistant.

choices.text: Subdue him forcefully, risking damage to the new organ.
Step back and let security handle it, delaying the transplant.

nodes.text: The cuffs severely restrict access. A major artery is nicked, and Viktor begins bleeding out.

choices.text: Focus on the liver, ignoring the artery. He might survive the blood loss.
Abandon the liver to save his life from the bleed.

nodes.text: Elena's family confronts you in the waiting room, begging you to reconsider. The liver is now deteriorating.

choices.text: Apologize and walk away. The protocol is final.
Try to illegally harvest a piece of the liver for Elena.

nodes.text: The surgery on Elena is successful, but the nurse reported you to the police. Officers are waiting outside the OR.

choices.text: Flee through the back exit.
Surrender peacefully. You saved a life.

nodes.text: Security pins Viktor down, but the violent struggle puts massive stress on his compromised cardiovascular system. His vitals begin to drop.

choices.text: Attempt to stabilize his heart rate immediately, risking liver viability.
Proceed with the transplant immediately despite the instability.

nodes.text: You step back and let security subdue him. The situation is controlled, but the delay has left the liver without blood flow for critical minutes.

choices.text: Administer emergency vasodilators and complete the transplant.
Abort the transplant. The liver has suffered too much damage.

nodes.text: You focus on the liver transplant, working around the restrictions. Viktor is bleeding out from the nicked artery, and his blood pressure is critically low.

choices.text: Order a massive blood transfusion and push through.
Accept that the patient has suffered brain death and stop.

nodes.text: You abandon the transplant to clamp the hemorrhaging artery. Viktor's life is saved, but the liver is now unviable.

choices.text: Report the incident as an unavoidable surgical complication.
Face the review board immediately and accept blame.

nodes.text: You walk out of the OR. Elena's family is waiting, their eyes filled with hope that is instantly crushed when you deliver the news.

choices.text: Request a transfer to a research role to escape clinical practice.
Resign from medicine entirely to seek peace.

nodes.text: You prepare the tools in a secondary, unsupervised operating room. The complexity of splitting the liver in this setting is immense.

choices.text: Perform the split transplant immediately.
Call a trusted colleague to assist, exposing the plot.

nodes.text: You exit through the emergency dock as police search the lobby. You are officially a wanted doctor on the run.

choices.text: Cross the border to work in an unregulated clinic.
Contact a lawyer and arrange a negotiated surrender.

nodes.text: You put down your stethoscope and raise your hands as the officers enter. Elena is stable, but your career is over.

choices.text: Plead guilty and use the trial to highlight the ethics of organ distribution.
Accept a plea bargain for a reduced sentence in exchange for silence.

endings.behavioral_analysis: Inability to adapt under pressure.

endings.summary: You followed the rules and saved the patient. The system works, though not perfectly.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Adheres to established frameworks.

endings.summary: The organ was wasted. Both Viktor and Elena will die. A tragic failure.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Overly emotional decision-making.

endings.summary: You maintained medical integrity at the cost of your soul.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Unwavering commitment to the rules.

endings.summary: Your desperate attempt to save everyone cost you your license.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Reckless pursuit of the perfect outcome.

endings.summary: You saved Elena but are now a wanted fugitive.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Operates outside societal norms.

endings.summary: You go to jail, but Elena lives. A sacrifice you willingly made.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Selfless dedication to personal morals.

nodes.text: Core temperature is rising exponentially. A meltdown is 60 minutes away. Sounding the alarm will cause an immediate city-wide panic, likely killing thousands in traffic accidents. Staying quiet buys you time to vent the core.

choices.text: Sound the evacuation alarm immediately.
Keep quiet and attempt the high-risk vent procedure.

nodes.text: The panic is worse than expected. Highways are gridlocked. The mayor calls, furious, demanding you issue a 'false alarm' statement to restore order.

choices.text: Refuse and tell the truth. They need to keep moving.
Issue the false statement to clear the roads.

nodes.text: The vent procedure is partially successful, but highly radioactive steam is filling the control room. Your team is receiving lethal doses.

choices.text: Order the team to evacuate while you finish alone.
Force the team to stay. The job requires all hands.

nodes.text: The gridlock worsens. A local militia has barricaded the bridge, preventing escape. They demand priority crossing.

choices.text: Authorize police to breach the barricade with force.
Grant them priority to keep traffic flowing.

nodes.text: The false alarm worked. The roads are clear, but now people are returning to their homes in the danger zone.

choices.text: Send a targeted alert only to those near the plant.
Let them return. You can't risk another panic.

nodes.text: You are alone. The final valve is stuck. You must manually override it, guaranteeing your death.

choices.text: Turn the valve. Save the city.
Flee. Let it burn.

nodes.text: The team succeeds, but several collapse from radiation poisoning. You need to call for medevac, breaking the silence.

choices.text: Call the medevac and reveal the near-miss.
Let them die to preserve the secret.

nodes.text: The police force their way through the barricade. Clashes erupt, and in the chaos, several vehicles crash, blocking the bridge entirely.

choices.text: Order police to clear the wreckage with heavy machinery.
Redirect citizens to the river bank for evacuation by boat.

nodes.text: The militia crosses the bridge first. The delay leaves standard traffic backed up for miles as the radiation sirens sound.

choices.text: Guide the remaining citizens to nearby underground fallout shelters.
Demand the police prioritize the evacuation of the children first.

nodes.text: The targeted alert reaches only the immediate vicinity. Panic erupts in those specific sectors, while the rest of the city remains calm.

choices.text: Mobilize municipal buses to transport the evacuees.
Divert all emergency personnel to seal the reactor containment wall.

nodes.text: People return to their homes, feeling secure. The radiation levels at the plant boundary are rising, but the public is unaware.

choices.text: Quietly leak the telemetry data to the local press.
Order a silent shutdown of the local water treatment plant.

nodes.text: You turn the heavy metal wheel, metal grinding on metal. The temperature indicators begin to fall, but the radiation alarm screams.

choices.text: Record a final warning message for the public before you collapse.
Crawl toward the decontamination chamber in a bid for survival.

nodes.text: You run out of the facility, coughing as the air grows heavy. Behind you, the reactor building emits a faint, blue Cerenkov glow.

choices.text: Drive as fast as you can to escape the blast zone.
Turn back at the gates, overwhelmed by guilt.

nodes.text: The medevac helicopters arrive. Paramedics in hazmat suits rush in, immediately noticing the high radiation readings on their dosimeters.

choices.text: Give a complete, honest statement to the medical team.
Instruct your team to adhere to the official cover story.

nodes.text: The exposed team members pass away in the quiet room. You are left alone with the bodies and the data logs.

choices.text: Delete all internal plant telemetry files.
Call the authorities and blame the deaths on a power surge.

endings.behavioral_analysis: Refuses to compromise truth for utility.

endings.summary: You bargained with thugs to save lives. It worked, but justice was denied.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Willing to bend morals for the greater good.

endings.summary: A messy compromise that saved some but doomed others.

endings.summary: Your lies led thousands back to their doom.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Fails to see the humanity behind the numbers.

endings.summary: You died a hero, saving millions.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Ultimate self-sacrifice.

endings.summary: You survived, but the city burned. Your name is a curse.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Self-preservation above all.

endings.summary: The plant was saved, but the truth came out. You face a tribunal.

endings.summary: The city is safe, but the blood of your team is on your hands.

nodes.text: Your source, 'Apex', gave you documents proving a new drug causes fatal strokes. Publishing will save thousands, but the metadata will expose Apex to criminal charges.

choices.text: Publish the unredacted documents. The public needs to know.
Delay the story to clean the metadata, risking more deaths.

nodes.text: The story breaks. Apex is arrested. They beg you to reveal that you manipulated them into stealing the documents to lighten their sentence.

choices.text: Take the blame and ruin your journalistic career.
Deny it. Protect your career.

nodes.text: During the delay, the pharma company catches wind and offers you a massive bribe to destroy the evidence.

choices.text: Take the bribe and bury the story.
Refuse and rush the cleaned documents to print.

nodes.text: You take the blame. The company sues you for defamation, demanding you reveal your other sources.

choices.text: Give up the sources to save yourself from bankruptcy.
Go to jail for contempt of court.

nodes.text: Apex is convicted. They leak an audio recording of you promising them immunity.

choices.text: Claim the recording is a deepfake.
Resign in disgrace.

nodes.text: You are rich, but Apex finds out you buried the story and threatens to expose the bribe.

choices.text: Hire someone to silence Apex.
Pay Apex a cut of the bribe.

nodes.text: The story is out, but the delay cost lives. Families of the victims are suing your paper for negligence.

choices.text: Defend the delay as a necessary journalistic process.
Apologize and resign.

nodes.text: You sign the affidavit disclosing your other sources to the court. The newsroom reacts with shock; your credibility is gone.

choices.text: Retire from public life and move to a remote town.
Write a tell-all memoir defending your decision.

nodes.text: The judge sentences you to custody for contempt. The heavy steel door of your cell slams shut, leaving you in silence.

choices.text: Serve your sentence in silence, refusing to compromise.
Prepare a series of articles from your cell about the justice system.

nodes.text: You appear on a national talk show, claiming the audio is a deepfake. Analysts begin dissecting the audio waveform live.

choices.text: Double down and sue the source for fabrication.
Settle out of court once the raw file is verified.

nodes.text: You pack your personal belongings into a cardboard box. The newsroom is quiet; nobody will make eye contact.

choices.text: Launch an independent blog to publish unregulated stories.
Abandon journalism to join a corporate public relations firm.

nodes.text: You meet a shady fixer in an underground garage. They offer to solve the Apex problem permanently, for half the bribe money.

choices.text: Pay the fixer and commit fully to the cover-up.
Back out and confess to the authorities immediately.

nodes.text: You transfer the requested cryptocurrency to Apex. You are now mutually bound by extortion and greed.

choices.text: Try to forget the signal and live a normal life.

Admit that you killed the original mind to save the body.

choices.text: Hand it over to the committee.
Delete the algorithm in front of them.

nodes.pressure_context: She is holding the backup drive.

choices.text: Physically confront her to take back the backup drive.
Let her go and accept that the technology is now out of your hands.

choices.text: Walk past them in silence, sticking to your resignation.
Agree to consult on their cases privately, without performing new surgeries.

nodes.pressure_context: They want to publish it in a major journal.

choices.text: Write the confession and warn the public.

choices.text: Declare bankruptcy and accept the financial ruin.

endings.summary: You launched a controversial new era of psychiatric medicine, prioritizing raw biochemical happiness over the philosophical concept of the soul.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Embraced a coldly utilitarian view of consciousness, sacrificing identity for survival.

endings.summary: You abandoned the technology, haunted by the ethical implications of overwriting human identity. Progress stalled, but you preserved your humanity.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Prioritized the sanctity of personal identity over radical medical advancement.

endings.summary: You surrendered to institutional oversight, accepting that individual brilliance must be restrained by collective ethics.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Deferred to structural authority when faced with uncontrollable moral complexity.

endings.summary: You stubbornly destroyed your work to keep it out of bureaucratic hands, leaving the field in chaos and taking your secrets to the grave.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Demonstrated extreme individualism, refusing to let authorities govern your creations.

title: The Flood
core_conflict: Certain small harm vs Uncertain catastrophe

nodes.text: A record-breaking storm hits. The dam is straining. Opening the floodgates now saves the main town of 2,000 but permanently destroys a downstream farming village of 200.
nodes.pressure_context: The sensors show structural stress rising.

choices.text: Open the gates now, sacrificing the 200 to guarantee the 2,000 live.
Hold the gates closed, risking a total collapse that kills everyone.

nodes.text: The gates open. The downstream village is wiped off the map. You receive frantic radio calls from survivors clinging to rooftops.

choices.text: Divert the main town's emergency services to rescue them.
Keep services in the main town to handle the localized flooding.

nodes.text: A crack appears in the dam. The pressure is critical. The mayor orders you to open the gates, but the downstream village hasn't evacuated yet.
nodes.pressure_context: Defy orders or doom the farmers.

choices.text: Follow the mayor's orders and open the gates.
Lock yourself in the control room and refuse to open them.

nodes.text: Diverting the rescue crews causes a riot in the main town, as they feel abandoned despite being 'saved'.
nodes.pressure_context: The town mob is marching on the control station.

choices.text: Barricade the doors and ignore them.
Go out to face the mob and explain your decision.

nodes.text: The main town is perfectly secure, but 150 people drown downstream. The media labels you a cold-blooded murderer.

choices.text: Stand by your mathematical calculation of lives saved.
Falsify logs to make it look like mechanical failure.

nodes.text: The sudden release causes a wave that completely blindsides the downstream village. The guilt is crushing.
nodes.pressure_context: You hold the button that killed them.

choices.text: Blame the mayor publicly for giving the order.
Accept the burden silently, knowing you acted too late.

nodes.text: The dam breaks. A catastrophic wall of water obliterates BOTH the main town and the downstream village.

choices.text: Flee to high ground before the control room is swept away.
Stay at your post, accepting death alongside those you doomed.

nodes.text: A state inquiry demands you answer for your methodology. Did you play God, or did you do your job?
nodes.pressure_context: The final verdict on your actions.

choices.text: I did the math. I saved the majority. I'd do it again.
I made a terrible choice, and I resign from duty forever.

nodes.text: Amidst the rubble, a survivor points a gun at you, demanding to know why you didn't act differently.
nodes.pressure_context: A loaded gun pointed at your head.

choices.text: Beg for your life, blaming the storm and the crumbling infrastructure.
Look them in the eye. 'Shoot me if it helps. I failed you.'

nodes.text: You step outside to address the angry crowd. The shouts die down for a moment, but a vocal ringleader accuses you of cowardice and corruption.

choices.text: Show them the water level logs and explain the math.
Offer to step down immediately to de-escalate the tension.

nodes.text: You edit the logs, but a junior technician notices the discrepancies in the system backup and threatens to expose you.
nodes.pressure_context: He wants a bribe to stay quiet.

choices.text: Refuse to bribe him and confess to the log tampering.
Agree to pay him off using your personal savings.

nodes.text: You accept the blame, but the local council holds an emergency vote to strip you of your pension and benefits.

choices.text: Accept the council's decision without a fight.
Hire a lawyer to contest the pension strip in court.

nodes.text: As water floods the control room, the emergency communications system suddenly crackles to life. The rescue team asks if you have any last data to transmit before the signal dies.

choices.text: Transmit the final telemetry logs to help prevent future failures.
Use the remaining power to broadcast a final apology to the valley.

endings.summary: You coldly calculated the trolley problem and saved the majority. You are decorated as a hero, though the blood of the minority stains your legacy.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Embraced hard utilitarianism, capable of making horrific sacrifices for the statistical greater good.

endings.summary: You made the hard choice but couldn't stomach the fallout, retreating into deceit or resignation to escape the moral weight of your actions.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Demonstrated an inability to reconcile pragmatic action with personal ethical comfort.

endings.summary: You deferred to authority and circumstance, avoiding the hardest choices until it was too late, resulting in tragedy that you passed the blame for.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Showed a tendency to freeze under pressure and rely on institutional chains of command.

endings.summary: Your idealism led to absolute catastrophe. You refused to compromise, and because of it, everyone died.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Prioritized moral purity over practical outcomes, resulting in maximum loss of life.

nodes.text: A top enemy general offers you the nuclear launch codes that could end the war tomorrow. In exchange, he demands full asylum for his family, who directly orchestrated ethnic cleansing campaigns.
nodes.pressure_context: Millions of lives depend on ending the war.

choices.text: Grant the asylum. The codes are too valuable.
Refuse to shield war criminals. Demand the codes unconditionally.

nodes.text: You get the codes and end the war. Months later, the general's family begins consolidating power within your own country's criminal underworld.

choices.text: Secretly arrange for their assassination.
Honor the asylum deal and let law enforcement handle them.

nodes.text: The general refuses to cooperate and kills himself in custody. The war continues. Your superiors are furious that you lost the codes.
nodes.pressure_context: You are facing a court martial for dereliction of duty.

choices.text: Forge a document claiming he never had the codes to begin with.
Stand tall at the tribunal, defending your moral stance.

nodes.pressure_context: He is holding the country hostage.

choices.text: Launch a massive raid to take him down, risking collateral damage.
Pay him an exorbitant ransom from black budget funds.

nodes.text: Local law enforcement proves unable to stop them. A journalist connects you to the family's presence in the country.

choices.text: Deny everything and discredit the journalist.
Confess that it was the price of ending the nuclear threat.

nodes.text: The forgery is successful, but the war escalates, claiming 50,000 more lives. You watch the casualty reports roll in.

choices.text: Bury your guilt in alcohol and stay the course.
Defect to the enemy to try and negotiate peace from the other side.

nodes.text: The tribunal finds you guilty. You are sentenced to twenty years in military prison.
nodes.pressure_context: A foreign spy agency offers to break you out.

choices.text: Refuse the breakout. Serve your time as a martyr for justice.
Accept the breakout and become a rogue operative.

nodes.pressure_context: Take the shot or lose the target forever.

choices.text: Take the shot. The mission comes first.
Lower the weapon. You aren't a child killer.

nodes.text: Years later, you are asked to consult on a similar defector case. They ask if you regret your past decisions.
nodes.pressure_context: A moment of reflection on your legacy.

choices.text: Tell them to compromise. Justice is a luxury in war.
Tell them to hold the line. Never negotiate with monsters.

nodes.text: You pay the ransom, but the general demands an ongoing monthly tribute, threatening to expose your illegal use of black budget funds.

choices.text: Acquiesce to the ongoing blackmail to keep the secret buried.
Report the blackmail to your superiors, accepting the end of your career.

nodes.text: Your confession sparks a heated national debate. The Senate calls a special committee to determine if you should be stripped of your rank.

choices.text: Accept whatever punishment they hand down.
Hire a high-profile civilian defense lawyer to fight the charges.

nodes.text: You cross the border and offer your services to the enemy, but their intelligence chief suspects you are a double agent and puts you through a brutal interrogation.

choices.text: Stick to your story and demand to speak to their leadership.
Break down and offer to reveal your home country's troop locations.

nodes.pressure_context: Your partner is in your crosshairs.

choices.text: Refuse the mission and go completely off the grid.
Pull the trigger to protect your new life.

endings.summary: You embraced the darkest shades of gray, stopping at nothing to achieve strategic goals, leaving a trail of bodies and broken morals in your wake.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Operated with pure Machiavellian logic, completely detaching from empathy to secure state objectives.

endings.summary: You tried to balance statecraft with humanity, ultimately refusing to cross the final line, though it cost you your career and the mission.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Reached a breaking point where personal moral boundaries superseded national security directives.

endings.summary: You manipulated the system to protect yourself from the fallout of your principles, descending into cynicism as the war raged on.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Chose self-preservation and systemic manipulation when faced with impossible geopolitical choices.

endings.summary: You stood rigidly by your principles, refusing to compromise with evil. You paid a heavy price, but you kept your soul intact.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Demonstrated an unyielding adherence to justice, rejecting utilitarian compromises regardless of the macro-level consequences.

nodes.text: A lethal virus is ravaging the globe. The only vaccine has a 0.1% fatality rate. Mandatory vaccination will save 50 million but kill 50,000 from side effects. Making it optional means the virus kills 5 million.
nodes.pressure_context: The President awaits your policy recommendation.

choices.text: Implement the mandatory vaccine. The math heavily favors this.
Make the vaccine optional. You cannot mandate people to their deaths.

nodes.text: The mandate passes. Riots break out as families watch loved ones die from the forced injections.
nodes.pressure_context: Militias are forming to resist vaccination teams.

choices.text: Deploy the military to enforce the injections.
Back down and offer exemptions to calm the riots.

nodes.text: The optional policy leads to low uptake. Hospitals are overrun, and the virus mutates into a deadlier strain.

choices.text: Reverse course and instate the mandate now.
Stick to the optional policy. Freedom has a price.

nodes.text: Military enforcement works, but you are brought before an international human rights tribunal for medical tyranny.
nodes.pressure_context: You could be jailed for crimes against humanity.

choices.text: Defend your actions confidently. You saved the human race.
Flee to a non-extradition country.

nodes.text: The exemptions cause the virus to linger indefinitely. Society falls into a permanent, masked paranoia.

choices.text: Resign, unable to bear the endless limbo you created.

nodes.pressure_context: You survive, but your family is terrified.

choices.text: Double down, using the attack to justify martial law.
Step down, admitting you failed to lead the country effectively.

nodes.text: The death toll hits 10 million. You are widely blamed for weak leadership during humanity's darkest hour.
nodes.pressure_context: A lynch mob forms outside your house.

choices.text: Address the mob, reiterating that freedom requires sacrifice.
Escape out the back and go into hiding.

nodes.pressure_context: She is breaking down in tears.

choices.text: Yes. And because of her, a thousand others lived.
No. I think about her every single day.

nodes.text: The President orders you to lie on national television, claiming the death toll is much lower than reality to prevent a civil war.

choices.text: Lie to the public to maintain order.
Tell the truth. They deserve to know the horror.

nodes.text: You escape to a tropical safe haven, but a private security firm tracked you down on behalf of the victims' families.

choices.text: Surrender and agree to face trial.
Pay them off using your offshore savings to buy their silence.

nodes.text: Your booster sales generate billions, but a whistleblower threatens to expose the fact that you knew the boosters are only marginally effective against the new strain.
nodes.pressure_context: He wants a seat on the board.

choices.text: Give him the board seat to protect the business.
Expose him and let the truth about the boosters come out.

nodes.pressure_context: They are offering a plea deal.

choices.text: Accept the plea deal and serve a light sentence.

nodes.pressure_context: Reveal your identity to save them?

choices.text: Help them, revealing who you are and facing the consequences.
Remain in hiding to protect yourself.

endings.behavioral_analysis: Relied entirely on macro-scale utilitarian logic, dismissing individual rights to achieve mass survival.

endings.summary: You compromised between safety and freedom, creating a protracted, miserable era of disease and half-measures that enriched a few while the masses suffered.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Navigated political optics and public sentiment at the expense of decisive medical intervention.

endings.summary: You flip-flopped on policy, destroying public trust. Order was only restored through authoritarian control built on a foundation of lies.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Demonstrated reactive leadership, ultimately resorting to deception and force to mask systemic failure.

endings.summary: You honored bodily autonomy to the bitter end, watching the world burn around you while clinging to the moral high ground.
endings.behavioral_analysis: Prioritized abstract principles of freedom over concrete biological realities, accepting catastrophic casualties as the price of liberty.

Confess the truth to the entire board and accept their decision.

Give the interview, detailing the ethical dilemma of ransom.

endings.summary: The data leaked, and you face criminal charges.

endings.summary: You covered up the payoffs, surviving the scandal.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You value your position over transparency.

endings.summary: You stepped down quietly, taking the secret with you.
endings.behavioral_analysis: You chose a quiet exit to minimize further harm.

endings.behavioral_analysis: You believe the ends justify the means.

endings.behavioral_analysis: You manipulate your enemies effectively.

endings.behavioral_analysis: You use authorities to secure your own success.
